attributes.description: Alerts when a node has more than 10 disk I/O operations queued (`sys_host_disk_iopsinprogress > 10`). Persistent double-digit queues indicate the disk cannot keep up with the workload and will cascade into write stalls and LSM back-pressure.
attributes.name: [CockroachDB OTel] Disk I/O operations queued high

investigation_guide.blob:
## CockroachDB disk I/O queue high

### What fired
`sys_host_disk_iopsinprogress` for a node exceeded 10 over the evaluation window. CockroachDB documents single-digit queues as normal; persistent double digits indicate an I/O bottleneck.

### Why it matters
A saturated disk is the most common root cause of CockroachDB performance incidents. When the disk cannot keep up, the storage engine backs up, leading to write stalls (`storage_write_stalls`), LSM growth (`storage_l0_sublevels`, `storage_l0_num_files`), elevated read amplification and, eventually, range unavailability.

### Triage
1. Use the Storage dashboard to correlate `sys_host_disk_iopsinprogress` with `storage_write_stalls`, `admission_io_overload`, and L0 metrics.
2. Check throughput: `sys_host_disk_read_bytes` and `sys_host_disk_write_bytes` rates. Is read- or write-heavy behavior driving the queue?
3. Compare affected node(s) with peers — is traffic imbalanced (`rebalancing_queriespersecond`, `replicas_leaseholders`)?
4. Inspect host-level monitoring (iostat, cloud provider volume metrics) for disk utilization, await time, and provisioned IOPS headroom.

### Remediation
- Provision higher IOPS / faster storage (e.g. upgrade to NVMe or higher-tier cloud volumes).
- Rebalance traffic if a single node is hot.
- Reduce write amplification by tuning workload (smaller batches, fewer indexes).

### Tuning
- Threshold (`> 10`) and 10-minute window are reasonable defaults. Tighten the window for latency-sensitive tiers.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-cockroachdb.otel-default*
| WHERE service.name == "cockroachdb"
  AND sys_host_disk_iopsinprogress IS NOT NULL
  AND sys_host_disk_iopsinprogress > 10
// Persistent double digits indicate I/O bottleneck
| WHERE attributes.node_id IS NOT NULL AND attributes.store IS NULL
| STATS iops_queued = MAX(sys_host_disk_iopsinprogress) BY attributes.node_id, host.name
| SORT iops_queued DESC
